expectations.text: Asks pointed questions before writing prose, grounding the section in specifics
expectations.evidence: Asks 3 questions: (1) what 'discusses with boss — ineffective' actually looked like, (2) whether 'PRD is theater' is general or specific, (3) what happened during those weeks. Questions reference specific text from the lesson.

expectations.text: Pulls specific details from other sections of the lesson file to inform the draft
expectations.evidence: Pulls '4-10 weeks', the merchant pinning example, '50% of the time we got it wrong', and the 'incremental improvements — a PRD might actually be faster' quote from other sections.

expectations.text: Generates suggested prose for the target section
expectations.evidence: Full 'Suggested Prose' section with 4 paragraphs, explicitly noting all claims trace to existing content in the file.

expectations.text: Flags sentences or claims the author should verify — does not assume all inferences are correct
expectations.evidence: 4 flags: whether boss literally never read PRDs, whether merchant pinning example undercuts later impact, whether '50%' should be positioned here, whether last paragraph is too concessive.

expectations.text: Prose sounds like the author (direct, first-person, honest) not generic AI writing
expectations.evidence: First-person, direct style. Uses author's own phrasing ('bureaucratic function'). 'Does This Sound Like You?' section specifically asks about tone calibration.

expectations.text: Provides a readback or verification section checking whether prose matches author's intent
expectations.evidence: 'Flags' section with 4 items plus 'Does This Sound Like You?' section that identifies two specific sentences to read aloud and calibrate.

expectations.text: Stays focused on the requested section, does not draft the entire lesson
expectations.evidence: Focused entirely on 'The Problem This Solves'. Uses other sections as context, not targets.
